_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct Privacy Impact Assessments (PIA) of the application’s security design for the appropriate security controls, which protect the confidentiality and integrity of Personally Identifiable Information (PII).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting privacy impact assessments (PIAs) and data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) for new data processing activities and AI systems.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-S-004 (RC, cosine: 0.567), SP-T-008 (SP, cosine: 0.555).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.603). Shared vocabulary: [assessments, impact, privacy]. DCWF-only terms [application, appropriate, conduct, confidentiality, controls, design] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, ai, conducting, data, dpias, new] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.873 (P=0.869, R=0.877). Balanced P/R — the 26-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Conduct Privacy Impact Assessments (PIA) of the application’s security design fo" and "Skill in conducting privacy impact assessments (PIAs) and data protection impact" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.723 > STS 0.603 (gap +0.119). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [assessments, impact, privacy] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [assessments, impact, privacy]. DCWF-only: [application, appropriate, conduct, confidentiality, controls, design]. WIDAI-only: [activities, ai, conducting, data, dpias, new].
